## [00:00 - 01:29]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer and today we're gonna talk about why it might be okay to republish your videos. Let's go! Music You should republish your underperforming videos. Recently I published a video that barely received any views at all. I'm both TikTok and Instagram. I'm used to videos having a few hundred views after a few hours. But in both cases the video had zero or close to zero views. These are the worst performing videos I've ever had. Around three to four hours after noticing this, I published them again to both platforms. One day later the TikTok video has over 1300 views and the Instagram reel has over 850 views. It's back to normal. There was nothing wrong with either video. For some reason I just didn't get any views. We'll often hear about algorithmic preference for unique content. But that obviously wasn't an issue here. I should also note that I scheduled both videos the way I always do. From the TikTok website and from Metabusiness Suite, both on desktop, I'm not using third-party apps. Bottom line, don't be afraid to republish an underperforming video. What do you think? So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
